sections.paragraph:
ἐκγαμίζω (Rhet. Gr. IV 247, 14.—Eustath. in Il. 9, 384 p. 758, 54 ἐγγαμίζειν οἱ ὕστερον λέγουσιν) to arrange marriage for someone, give in marriage, as v.l. in Mt 24:38 and 1 Cor 7:38 (here twice). Pass. be given in marriage Mt 22:30; Lk 17:27; 20:35, everywhere as v.l. Except for the Mt passages, most edd. read γαμίζω (q.v. 1).—M-M.

Eustath Eustath , XII a.d.—List 5

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

edd. edd. = editions, editors

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 300
